title: Benchmarking Bioemu-1: Establishing a Baseline for Protein Structural Ensemble Prediction

content:
2025 Honors Dean’s High Impact Award for Undergraduate Research Recognized for outstanding contributions to undergraduate research at Iowa State University, with emphasis on innovation, rigor, and mentorship of peers to enhance research outcomes.
Best-In-Show, George Washington Carver Poster Challenge Awarded top recognition for research poster presentation, selected based on clarity, impact, and originality of research work.
Borlaug Poster Competition - 1st Place (Undergraduate Division) Recognized by the Iowa State University College of Agriculture and Life Sciences for research exploring how AI and FinTech empower entrepreneurs addressing global food and sustainability challenges.
Bangladesh–Sweden Trust Fund Travel Grant Competitive international grant supporting research travel and presentation opportunities, enabling participation in global conferences and networking with international researchers.
ISU LAS Travel Grant ($400) Awarded by the College of Liberal Arts and Sciences to support presentation at AIChE 2025 Annual Meeting and engagement with leading experts in the field.
2024 Honors Dean’s List (Fall 2024) Achieved distinction for academic excellence across all courses while maintaining high GPA while engaging in research and extracurricular activities.
These recognitions reflect my commitment to rigorous research, innovation, and contributions to the academic and research community at Iowa State University and beyond.

Recognized for outstanding contributions to undergraduate research at Iowa State University, with emphasis on innovation, rigor, and mentorship of peers to enhance research outcomes.
title: Awards & Recognition
Federated In-Context Prompt Selection for Multi-Modal 3D Dental Imaging: A Theoretical Framework with Privacy-Preserving Guarantees

content:
Overview Collaborated with Professor Ryan McGhee at the ISU Department of Agricultural and Biosystems Engineering to develop a versatile chatbot frontend that significantly improves user experience and interface design.
Key Responsibilities Frontend development and architecture User interface design and enhancement User experience optimization Integration with backend systems Iterative design based on user feedback Skills Applied Modern web frameworks and technologies Responsive UI design User-centered design principles Software development best practices
summary: Developed a versatile chatbot frontend to improve user experience and interface design for agricultural and biosystems engineering applications.
title: Chatbot Frontend Development & UI Enhancement

content:
Overview Developed novel approaches to enhance the safety and ethical compliance of medical LLM outputs. This research addresses critical challenges in deploying AI for healthcare:
Multi-agent evaluation frameworks using specialized LLMs as evaluator agents Iterative refinement against established healthcare standards (AMA-9 Principles) Safety Risk Assessment (SRA-5) tier implementation Byzantine-resilient aggregation with differential privacy guarantees Key Achievements ACM-BCB 2025 Presentation - Poster presentation at The 16th ACM Conference on Bioinformatics, Computational Biology, and Health Informatics Developed framework pairing generative models (DeepSeek R1, Med-PaLM) with evaluator agents (LLaMA 3.1, Phi-4) Focus on real-world deployment challenges and regulatory compliance
summary: Developing multi-agent frameworks to ensure medical AI systems are safe, ethical, and compliant with healthcare standards and regulations.
title: Medical AI Safety & Ethical Alignment

content:
Overview Exploring how state-of-the-art vision-language models (VLMs) can be adapted and optimized for specialized agricultural tasks. This research contributes to advancing precision agriculture through AI.
Research Focus Benchmarking VLMs on agricultural datasets Specialized prompt engineering for farm-related tasks Cross-modal understanding of agricultural imagery Real-world deployment in agricultural systems Publications WACV 2025 - “Leveraging Vision Language Models for Specialized Agricultural Tasks” (IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision) Research on protein structural prediction (Bioemu-1 benchmarking at AIChE 2025) Applications Crop disease and pest identification Yield estimation Field condition assessment Climate-aware agricultural decision support
summary: Leveraging vision-language models and computer vision to address specialized challenges in agricultural applications, including crop monitoring, disease detection, and yield prediction.
title: Vision-Language Models for Agriculture

content:
Overview Collaborating with Professor Soumik Sarkar at the Translational AI Center to explore how LLMs can enhance decision-making processes in finance. This research includes:
Analyzing how LLMs interpret market signals and business fundamentals Studying investor behavior through the lens of AI analysis (Shark Tank data) Developing frameworks for AI-assisted financial analysis Understanding market orientation and brand storytelling effects on investment decisions Key Contributions Published research on market orientation and startup pitch evaluation (JBIM, REU 2025) Developed LLM-based analytical frameworks for entrepreneurship and investment analysis Presented findings at TrAC Day 2025 and REU Summer Symposium 2025
summary: Leveraging Large Language Models to improve financial decision-making, with applications to startup evaluation and investor behavior analysis.
title: LLMs in Finance: Enhancing Decision-Making Processes
